channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: US, Colombia clash over deportations and raise tariffs in show of Trump's pledge to limit migration
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — The United States and Colombia, long close partners in anti-narcotics efforts, clashed Sunday over the deportation of migrants and imposed tariffs on each other’s goods in a show of what countries could face if they intervene in the Trump administration’s crackdown on illegal
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Hegseth takes charge at the Pentagon, here's what changes could be in store
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth starts his first official day on Monday morning, he will face a daunting array of issues to tackle — from global conflicts and border security to administrative tasks.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombian leader quickly caves after Trump threats, offers presidential plane for deportation flights
item.description: Gustavo Petro was quickly brought to heel by President Donald Trump after the Colombian leader rejected several deportation flights from the U.S. He is now offering his presidential plane to help with the flights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sen. Marshall 'absolutely' comfortable with Trump’s purge of inspectors general
item.description: Sen. Roger Marshall (R-Kan.) suggested that, like Reagan, Trump could potentially rehire some of the watchdog officials after cleaning house at the agencies.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Secretary of State Rubio hails release of US prisoner in Belarus as controversy hangs over nation's election
item.description: President Donald Trump's new Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced that a U.S. citizen imprisoned in Belarus under Joe Biden has since been released.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants Jordan and Egypt to accept more Palestinian refugees and floats plan to 'clean out' Gaza
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump said he would like to see Jordan, Egypt and other Arab nations increase the number of Palestinian refugees they are accepting from the Gaza Strip, potentially moving out enough of the population to “just clean out” the war-torn area to create a virt
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's federal DEI purge puts hundreds on leave, nixes $420M in contracts
item.description: Since President Trump signed an EO to rid the government of DEI programs, the administration says 395 workers have been placed on leave and around $420 million in related contracts have been canceled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's DOJ asks Supreme Court to freeze cases on student debt, environment
item.description: President Trump’s Justice Department on Friday asked the Supreme Court to freeze four pending cases implicating the environment or student debt.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Bessent nomination to lead Treasury
item.description: The Senate on Saturday voted to advance Scott Bessent’s nomination to become the next secretary of the Treasury, putting him in line to be confirmed on Monday. Senators voted 67-23 to tee up final passage on Bessent’s nomination. The final vote will take place Monday evening at 5:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: White House fires some independent inspectors general
item.description: NewsNation partner The Hill reported that Trump has ousted around 17 federal watchdogs within various departments
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: He's emboldened, he's organized and he's still Trump: Takeaways from the president's opening days
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s first week in office isn't over yet, but already it offers signals about how his next four years in the White House may unfold.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic states weigh more support for immigrants as Trump administration cracks down
item.description: The resistance efforts in California, New York and other states are a counterpoint to the many Republican-led states.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Florida schools will comply with ICE on campuses: Education department
item.description: Schools across America, including in Florida, are gearing up for the possibility of officials with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) showing up to their campuses.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump leans in on targeting Russian oil revenue as he tries to fulfill pledge to end Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is emphasizing that targeting Russia's oil revenue is the best way to get Moscow to end its nearly three-year war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Experts warn about the ’crumbling infrastructure’ of federal government data
item.description: Unstable federal funding puts at risk the government statistics used to track the U.S. economy and population, officials and data users warn. That’s before any cuts by President Trump and Congress.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: Early favorite emerges in Republican primary for NJ governor: poll
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – A candidate has emerged as an early favorite in the Republican primary for New Jersey governor, a new Emerson College Polling/PIX11/The Hill poll shows.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Stick with us: Why the internet is obsessed with one of the simplest tools known to man
item.description: "Stick Nation" is a worldwide movement that thrives on irreverent, fantastical and downright wholesome descriptions of sticks.
item.dc:creator: Alina Hartounian, MPR News

item.title: Bishop Mariann Budde, former Minnesota bishop, tells NPR ‘I won’t apologize’ for sermon addressing Trump
item.description: The Episcopal bishop of Washington spoke with NPR to discuss her sermon addressing President Trump and why she asked him to have mercy.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise, MPR News

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to investigate local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump wants to pull US out of World Health Organization again; here's what may happen next
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump fires 4 presidential appointees, including his former Iran envoy
item.description: President Donald announced the firing of four high-profile presidential appointees just after midnight Tuesday, including a top envoy to Iran during his first term and retired Gen. Mark Milley, who Trump tapped as Joint Chiefs chairman in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Florida Republicans express optimism, state Democrat chair urges caution after inauguration
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) – Many Florida Republicans in the state legislature traveled to the nation’s capital for Inauguration Day events. Lawmakers like Sen. Rick Scott who works on Capitol Hill posted a video statement on X congratulating President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump to issue 10 executive orders cracking down on border
item.description: President-elect Trump is set to sign off on 10 executive orders Monday, moving swiftly to crack down on immigration and drug cartels during his first day in office with a blitzkrieg of immigration actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
